Off Calendar Dec-05-2016 Continued To Dec-20-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

In re: Defendants Billfloat, Inc., Ryan Gilbert And Sean O'Malley's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Form Interrogatories - Per CCP 639(a)(5), (c), (d)(2) and (d)(6), the court on its own motion has decided to appoint a referee for all discovery purposes in this case. The exceptional circumstances requiring the appointment of a discovery referee are the large number of discovery disputes that the parties have already had in this case and the large amount of paperwork that the parties have filed in support of and in opposition to motions to compel. The court further finds that the parties have the economic ability to pay their pro rata share of the referee's fees. If the parties have agreed on a discovery referee, one or both sides should so notify the court by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org by 4pm on December 2, 2016 and, in that event, the parties need not appear at the hearing on December 5, 2016 and counsel for Golden Pacific Bank is required to prepare a proposed order entitled "Order Appointing Discovery Referee" which states verbatim the above portions of this tentative ruling as well as that all pending and future discovery motions will be referred to the referee. If the parties have not agreed on a discovery referee, there will be a single discovery referee and each side may nominate up to three persons by filing a list of nominees no later than December 12, 2016 and may object to the persons nominated by the other side no later than December 15, 2016 and the hearing will be continued to December 20, 2016 for the appointment of the referee and the referral of all pending and future discovery motions to the referee.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings By Defendants Ryan Gilbert And Sean O?Malley
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS RYAN GILBERT and SEAN O'MALLEY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED in its entirety. The fraud cause of action is alleged with sufficient particularity and is not barred by the parties' contracts. Plaintiff Golden Pacific Bank may seek both tort and contractual recovery based on the same alleged misrepresentation. "An action for promissory fraud may lie where a defendant fraudulently induces the plaintiff to enter into a contract. [citations] In such cases, the plaintiff's claim does not depend upon whether the defendant's promise is ultimately enforceable as a contract. 'If it is enforceable, the [plaintiff] ... has a cause of action in tort as an alternative at least, and perhaps in some instances in addition to his cause of action on the contract.'" (Lazar v. Sup. Crt. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 638; see also Riverisland Cold Storage, Inc. v. Fresno-Madera Prod. (2013) 55 Cal.4th 1169 (parol evidence rule may not be used as a shield to prevent the proof of fraud even if the evidence shows a promise directly at a variance with the promise of the writing).) The trade secret cause of action is well-pleaded. Paragraph 57 of the complaint sufficiently identifies the alleged trade secrets. Defendants' arguments raise factual issues that are not amenable on a non-evidentiary motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jun-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF GOLDEN PACIFIC BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER ONSTOTT OF KRONICK LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-916-321-4500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT;
ATTY. WILLIAM T. WEBB, TEL. NO. 1-415-277-7200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. ROBERT I. LOCKWOOD OF THE HINSHAW LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-263-8130 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.
RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff and cross-defendant Golden Pacific Bank's motion to change venue is denied. With the dismissal of the Contra Costa case, there has been a significant change of circumstances that warrants re-evaluation of all the many pertinent factors bearing on this motion. The grounds for denying the motion are a combination of the following: 1) there is no litigation involving the parties pending in any county other than this county; 2) the Sacramento Superior Court determined that this action was not properly filed in Sacramento County and transferred this action to this county; 3) the normal rule is that the complaint, not the cross-complaint, determines venue, and there is no reason to depart from that rule here; 4) Billfloat initiated the litigation between the parties by filing an action in this county; 5) Billfloat's filing of that first lawsuit in this county established a priority of sorts that litigation between Golden Pacific Bank and Billfloat was properly venued in this county and that priority was maintained and respected by this court when the first motion to change venue was denied in case number 447679; and 6) maintaining this action in this county does not run afoul of the reasons proffered for any of the prior venue rulings made by this court or any other court. Taking all of those considerations together, the "ends of justice" tip in favor of retaining this action in this county because, although there has arguably been venue gamesmanship on both sides, this action, as presently constituted for venue purposes, is closer to a combination of the case that was transferred from Sacramento Superior Court and the initial complaint in this county in case number 547679 for which a motion for change of venue was denied than either the state of the lawsuit in case number 547679 at the time that a motion to change venue was granted or the case for which the Contra Costa Superior Court granted the motion to change venue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/hk
